name: MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup
section_which_is_best_for_you:
Finding the ideal tool for local SEO can be challenging with so many options available. In this article, we’ll compare MozLocal, Yext, and Synup to highlight how each platform handles local listings, reputation management, Local SEO, and other functionalities in general. 

We’ll break down their core features and performance to help you determine which tool offers the best solution for managing and improving your local search presence.

MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup: At a Glance!

MozLocal provides fundamental tools for managing local listings and reputation, ensuring that your business information is accurate and consistent across directories. It also offers basic reputation management features, allowing you to track and respond to customer reviews. However, it falls short in offering comprehensive local SEO and analytical features, which may limit its effectiveness for more advanced local marketing needs.

Yext is often a preferred choice among marketers, offering a comprehensive toolset that includes listings, reviews, social media management, store locator pages, and more. It's user-friendly and boasts excellent integrations, though the cost can be high for advanced features.

Synup offers an all-in-one solution for managing local business online presence effectively and affordably. It simplifies Local SEO management while also catering to social media needs, all at a nominal cost. With Synup, businesses can ensure accurate listings across various online directories, monitor reviews from platforms like Google and Yelp, and gain valuable insights to enhance local SEO performance and competitive edge. The platform assists in managing citations, resolving duplicate listings, and optimizing Google My Business profiles, thereby boosting local visibility and fostering better customer engagement. It is suitable for businesses of all sizes and is extremely agency-focused. 

‍

‍
section_listing_management:
MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup: Listings Management 

Source: MozLocal, G2

MozLocal offers basic listing management capabilities such as keeping your listings updated across Google, Facebook, and other search engines. Along with deleting duplicate listings if any. Helps with understanding how to optimize your listings and profile better along with automating the distribution of your local listings across partner sites. When looking at things that are not so great about Moz, it does not support bulk verification which might be a bit of a hassle. 

Yext has an extensive publisher network, covering over 200 directories and search engines. It provides a wide range of services under listing management. However, switching to a different tool could lead to the loss of 50-60% of your data, requiring significant rework from scratch.

Synup provides an entirely API-based listings management system, making it highly efficient and scalable for businesses of all sizes. One of its standout features is its ability to handle bulk verification, which simplifies the process of managing multiple listings across various platforms. Unlike some other services, Synup does not claim ownership of your listings, which means you retain full control over your data. This is particularly beneficial if you decide to migrate to a different platform in the future, as you won't lose any of your valuable business information during the transition. Synup's approach ensures that your listings remain accurate, consistent, and fully under your control at all times.

MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup: Reputation Management 

MozLocal comes with a reputation management system for its premium users and offers some basic features like responding to reviews and monitoring them, a single-interface dashboard, alerts, and notifications, and helps monitor review sentiment, common keywords in reviews, and customer feedback over time. This almost sums it up. Users feel that its a quite basic and can do a little bit with more add-on features. 

Source: Yext

Yext offers an all-in-one reputation management system with features like Generative Review Response, and first-party review generation feedback among many others. These tools help businesses manage and enhance their online reputation, however, many users are hesitant to sign up due to Yext's high fees and the perception that their customer support is impersonal and unresponsive at most times. 

Source: Synup

Synup offers a fully customizable, AI-enabled review management system designed to streamline reputation management for agencies, resellers, SMBs, and other businesses. This system facilitates the generation of new responses and includes customizable templates and review responses, making it easy to maintain a consistent and professional tone. Its smart response system uses AI to simplify the process of replying to reviews, ensuring timely and appropriate engagement with customers. Additionally, Synup's pricing is fair and accessible, making it an attractive option for those seeking to enhance their online reputation without breaking the bank. This combination of advanced features and affordability makes Synup a valuable tool for managing and improving customer feedback efficiently.

MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup: Local SEO 

MozLocal doesn’t help with building store locator or search pages, both which are a crucial part of your local search journey. 

Yext offers features tailored for enterprises and large-scale businesses, including Rank Tracker, Store Locator Pages, and an AI-powered chatbot for landing pages. Users also feel that the Yext Pages is a great feature to have and is incredibly easy to use. 

Source: Yext, G2

Synup provides comprehensive Local SEO solutions for businesses of all sizes, offering a range of powerful features to enhance online visibility and engagement. Key features include a keyword rank tracker to monitor search engine performance, a grid rank tracker for detailed local SEO insights, and customizable store locator pages to improve local search presence. Additionally, Synup offers a voice-search optimizer to ensure your business is easily found through voice search and an integrated social media platform for streamlined management of social accounts.

Source: Synup, G2

One standout feature that distinguishes Synup from competitors is its hyper-localized campaigns. These campaigns enable businesses to run targeted promotions and offers based on specific triggers such as weather changes or festive occasions, allowing for highly relevant and timely marketing efforts. This unique capability ensures that businesses can connect with their audience in a more personalized and impactful way.

MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup: Local Analytics 

Synup provides thorough local analytics, offering all the data points needed to evaluate your business or clients' performance. This includes listing analytics like profile views, phone calls, and website visits, along with local rank tracking for keywords and grid ranks. However, Synup's reporting and visualization features could be improved.

Source: Synup

Local analytics isn't Yext's strong point. While they offer excellent SEO and listing analytics capabilities tailored for enterprise needs, they fall short in providing location-level or rank-tracking features. These capabilities are vital for understanding and improving local search performance, and their absence can be a significant drawback for businesses focusing on local visibility.

MozLocal doesn’t provide any location-level analytics. It does provide basic listing and review analytics, but that does not quite cut it out.

MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup: Reseller Support 

Mozlocal and Yext, both don’t offer reseller support options. Although Yext offers integration and partnership programs for agencies, consultants, and other SaaS resellers/platforms, the support after partnering is limited. Yext focuses more on the enterprise segment, which means they invest less in helping their partners grow their businesses.

‍

Synup is the premier choice for reseller support, offering a comprehensive suite of tools and resources. These include white-labeling, client listing management, reputation management, and lead generation, ensuring your agency operates seamlessly. Additionally, Synup features a white-label business scan tool to capture and integrate leads directly into your website, and it provides managed services for your customers.

MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup: Pricing 

Synup offers a more affordable alternative to Yext while delivering many of the same functionalities. Users often consider Synup a great value for money due to its comprehensive solutions and responsive support team ready to address any questions or concerns. With Synup, you get a cost-effective solution that maintains high quality and service standards.

Yext is not very consistent with its pricing module. Its plans keep changing and are mostly suited for enterprise-level or large organizations. 

MozLocal is affordably priced, starting its basic plan at $14 Per Month, however, it only offers basic listing management features along with review monitoring and charges extra for additional reputation management features. 

MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup: Which One Should You Choose?

Synup is the clear winner here! With its affordable pricing and one-size-fits-all, it fulfills the needs and requirements of businesses of any size, be it a small company, agency, freelancer, or enterprise. Synup’s resources not only enhance the reseller experience but also empower agencies to deliver exceptional service to their clients, making Synup a valuable partner in driving growth and success.

Yext is great for enterprise-level businesses that don’t mind spending. However, it’s worthy noting that its’s support is below sub-par and focuses mostly on enterprise clients and requests that are high priority. 

MozLocal is great for businesses that have a tight budget and are looking for basic capabilities such as listings management and review monitoring features. However, customer support might be lacking in a few areas. 

‍

In Conclusion

In summary, if you need a solution that excels in third-party integrations, offers strong after-sales support, and is budget-friendly, Synup is an outstanding choice. It provides valuable managed services and white-labeling options, making it perfect for resellers and agencies. Additionally, Synup ensures secure data migration, so you don’t have to worry about losing information when switching services. When compared to Yext and MozLocal, Synup tops the chats.
faq_rich_text:
MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup: FAQs

  1. What is MozLocal used for?

MozLocal helps businesses manage their local online presence by ensuring accurate and consistent location data across directories and search engines. It simplifies directory submissions, monitors listing performance, and aggregates data to minimize errors. 

  1. What is Moz best for?

Moz is best for listings management, helping businesses maintain accurate and consistent location data across various online directories and platforms. It simplifies the process of updating and managing local listings to improve visibility and local SEO.

  1. Why use Yext?

Yext helps businesses manage their online listings and location data across many platforms. It ensures consistent information, boosts visibility, and provides useful analytics to track performance.

‍

seo.title: MozLocal v/s Yext v/s Synup, Which One Should You Choose in 2024?
seo.description: Confused between MozLocal, Yext, and Synup for your Local SEO needs? This article might just have an answer for you!
